title: Day 18 Quiz — Authentication and Protected Routes
description: Verify JWT basics, storage APIs, auth types, route guards, and session flow.

questions.prompt: JWT payload is encrypted and cannot be read by the client.
questions.explanation: JWT payload is base64-encoded, not encrypted — anyone can decode and read claims. Signature prevents tampering.

questions.prompt: What happens to sessionStorage when the browser tab closes?

options.text: Data is cleared for that tab

questions.explanation: sessionStorage is scoped to the tab session.

questions.prompt: What does ProtectedRoute typically render for unauthenticated users?

options.text: The protected page with hidden content

questions.explanation: Route guards redirect unauthenticated users to login, often preserving return URL in location state.

questions.prompt: Which are parts of a JWT? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: JWT structure is header.payload.signature — three dot-separated segments.

questions.prompt: Attach bearer token in RTK Query via {{blank1}} in fetchBaseQuery.
questions.explanation: prepareHeaders mutates request headers before each API call.

questions.prompt: What does localStorage.getItem return for missing key?
questions.explanation: getItem returns null when the key does not exist.

questions.prompt: Authentication vs authorization — which verifies permissions?
questions.explanation: Authentication proves identity; authorization checks what that identity can access.

questions.prompt: Complete the redirect with return location.
questions.explanation: location state preserves intended destination for post-login redirect.

questions.prompt: localStorage vs httpOnly cookie for JWT — one tradeoff each.
questions.sampleAnswer: localStorage is accessible to JavaScript — vulnerable to XSS stealing tokens but immune to CSRF. httpOnly cookies cannot be read by JS — safer from XSS but need CSRF protection. Production often prefers httpOnly cookies.
questions.explanation: Interviewers expect awareness of both attack vectors.

questions.prompt: Which belong in a typed AuthState? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: AuthState tracks user, token, and auth lifecycle — not unrelated domain data.
